info.name: Declut API
info.description:
Declut — trust-first escrow marketplace API for buying/selling pre-owned household items. NestJS + MongoDB. Payment is held in escrow until a buyer-held confirmation code is entered by the seller at the meetup.

AUTH PATTERN (regular users): every protected route expects `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`. Run Auth → Register (email + password + phone) or Auth → Login (identifier = email OR phone, + password) first — both auto-save `accessToken`/`refreshToken` into collection variables via a test script, so every other request in this collection picks them up automatically. When the access token expires (15m default), run Auth → Refresh Token. Register also auto-saves an `otpToken` for Auth → Verify Email (signup OTP) — verifying flips `emailVerified` to true, which KYC → Verify Identity requires before it will run. Forgot-password/OTP/reset-password tokens are the same kind of stateless JWT the client carries between steps — never stored server-side — and also auto-save into `otpToken`/`resetToken` via the Auth folder's requests.

ADMIN: Admin is a WHOLLY SEPARATE collection/identity from User, with its own JWT secrets — a regular accessToken will never work on an admin route, and vice versa. Bootstrap the first admin with `npm run seed:admin -- <email> <password> [name]` (see CLAUDE.md), then use Admin Auth → Admin login, which auto-saves `adminAccessToken`/`adminRefreshToken`. The Admin folder (acting on the marketplace) and Admin Auth folder (managing admin accounts themselves) both use `{{adminAccessToken}}`.

REALTIME: admin-only live notifications (new dispute, stalled transaction) are delivered over a WebSocket (Socket.IO) gateway, not plain HTTP — see the Realtime (Admin WebSocket) folder for connection details, since Postman's standard REST collection format can't execute a Socket.IO connection directly.

Every HTTP route lives under /api (e.g. /api/auth/login) — the admin WebSocket gateway is the one exception, since setGlobalPrefix only affects the HTTP router, not Socket.IO namespaces. Set the `baseUrl` collection variable to wherever the API is running, including the /api prefix (defaults to http://localhost:3000/api).

request.description: Basic liveness + Mongo connectivity check. No auth required.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Google sign-in (google-auth-library, no Firebase) + email/password (email + password + phone), both converging on backend-issued JWT access/refresh tokens. Login accepts either email or phone as the identifier. Email/password signup requires OTP email verification before KYC — see Verify Email below. Password reset/OTP and email-verification tokens are stateless JWTs — never stored in the DB — see CLAUDE.md's "Password Reset & OTP Flow" and "Signup Flow" sections.

request.description: Creates an email/password account. phone is a Nigerian number (07/08/09 + 9 digits local, or +234 international) — it doubles as the alternate login identifier and must be unique. Always a plain User — admin accounts are a wholly separate collection/flow (see the Admin Auth folder), never created here. Immediately sends a 6-digit email-verification OTP (see Verify Email below) and returns it as a stateless otpToken alongside the session tokens. Rate-limited. Auto-saves accessToken/refreshToken/otpToken to collection variables.

identifier accepts either an email or a phone number (whichever the account was registered with) — set it to "08012345678" to log in by phone instead. Generic "invalid credentials" on either a missing user or wrong password. Rate-limited. Auto-saves accessToken/refreshToken.

Confirms the 6-digit code emailed at registration and flips emailVerified to true. Unlike the forgot-password OTP chain, this is JwtAuthGuard-protected (not stateless/anonymous) — the caller already has a session from Register, so there's no enumeration concern to defend against. The mobile app should move the user into KYC verification right after this succeeds. Rate-limited.
Issues a fresh OTP + otpToken if the caller's email isn't verified yet (short-circuits with "Email is already verified" and no send attempt if it already is). Auto-saves the new otpToken.
idToken is verified server-side against Google's public keys via google-auth-library (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID as audience) — Firebase is never involved. Rate-limited. Auto-saves accessToken/refreshToken.
Rotates both tokens — the presented refresh token is revoked and a new pair issued. A revoked/expired/reused token is rejected. Rate-limited. Auto-saves the new accessToken/refreshToken.
Revokes the presented refresh token's session row. Does not affect other logged-in devices.

Sends a 6-digit OTP via email (Brevo) and returns a stateless otpToken — nothing is stored server-side; the token itself carries a bcrypt hash of the OTP, verified in Verify OTP below. Same generic response whether or not the email is registered (anti-enumeration). Rate-limited (5/min). Auto-saves otpToken.
Issues a brand new OTP + otpToken for the same account the current otpToken was for. Rate-limited (5/min). Auto-saves the new otpToken.

Verifies the 6-digit code against the hash embedded in otpToken. On success, returns a resetToken (also stateless — its single-use property comes from a fingerprint of the CURRENT password hash embedded in it, not a DB lookup). Rate-limited (5/min). Auto-saves resetToken.

Consumes resetToken — verified against a fingerprint of the account's CURRENT password hash, so the token self-invalidates the instant the password changes (reusing it afterward correctly 401s). Revokes all of the account's existing refresh tokens (forces re-login everywhere). Rate-limited (5/min).
Authenticated equivalent of reset-password — verifies currentPassword instead of an OTP flow. Also revokes all other sessions' refresh tokens.

item.name: Users
item.description: Profile, KYC status, cached trust score and rating stats.

request.description: Full private profile — email, kycStatus, trustScore, bank details, etc. Only ever returned to the user themselves.
All fields optional. Bank details are required before a seller can accept a checkout (used to create a Paystack subaccount).
Public-safe subset only: name, verified (boolean, from kycStatus), trustScore, avgRating, reviewCount. No email, no bank details.

item.name: Listings
item.description: Create/edit/archive/delete, radius + keyword search. All routes require login (no guest browsing in v1).

request.description: Backend never touches image bytes — returns a short-lived signed payload the mobile app uses to upload directly to Cloudinary, then sends the resulting URLs back in Create Listing's images field.

All query params optional. Supplying lat/lng runs a $geoNear radius search (default radius DEFAULT_SEARCH_RADIUS_KM); keyword alone ranks by text relevance; neither param falls back to newest-first. Remove query params you don't need.
category: furniture|electronics|appliances|kitchenware|clothing|decor|books|other. condition: new|like_new|good|fair|poor. location is {lat, lng}, not GeoJSON — the service converts it internally.
Owner-only (403 otherwise). All fields from Create Listing are optional here.
Owner-only. Removes it from search without deleting it.
Owner-only. Soft delete (status: deleted) — never a real Mongo delete, since a Transaction may reference this listing.

item.name: Favorites
item.description: Buyer save/unsave listings, list saved listings.

request.description: Idempotent upsert — favoriting an already-favorited listing is a no-op, not an error.

item.name: KYC
item.description: NIN + facial liveness, gated at first listing (sellers) / first purchase (buyers) — not mandatory at signup.

request.description: Requires emailVerified: true (400 otherwise) — per the intended signup flow, verify email before KYC. NIN + facial liveness via QoreID (behind a KycProvider interface — swappable vendor). The selfie image is held in memory only long enough to forward to the provider; never persisted. Sets kycStatus to pending, then verified/rejected (or reverts to unverified on a provider/config error, not a false rejection). Rate-limited (5/min) given the payload size and cost per call.
Audit trail only — pass/fail + reference ID per attempt, never images.

item.name: Offers
item.description: Buyer offers below asking price; seller accepts/rejects/counters. An accepted offer seeds a Transaction at its amount instead of list price.

request.description: Offers where you're the buyer or the seller.
Only visible to the buyer/seller on that offer.
Can't offer on your own listing. One pending offer per (buyer, listing) at a time (409 on a second). Auto-expires after OFFER_EXPIRY_DAYS if untouched.
Only the party who did NOT propose the current amount can accept/reject/counter — it must be "their turn". An accepted offer's amount is what Create Transaction uses instead of list price.
Terminal-status's the current offer (status: countered) and creates a new offer in the chain (parentOffer points back), with proposedBy flipped and a fresh expiry.
Only the current proposer can withdraw, and only while pending.

item.name: Transactions & Payments
item.description: Paystack checkout → escrow → buyer-held confirmation code → seller-triggered release, 10% commission at release. See the webhook note before trying to call it manually.

request.description: Initializes a Paystack transaction (held via subaccount split, manual settlement) and returns paystackAuthorizationUrl for the mobile app to open. offerId is optional — omit it to buy outright at list price, or pass an accepted offer's ID to check out at the negotiated amount. One pending checkout per (buyer, listing) at a time. Rate-limited.
Transactions where you're the buyer or seller. confirmationCode is only ever included for the buyer, and only while status is escrow_active/awaiting_inspection.
Only visible to the buyer/seller on that transaction.

Seller-only. Correct code triggers a Paystack payout to the seller minus commission and marks the transaction completed. Wrong code increments a counter; hitting MAX_CODE_ATTEMPTS auto-flags the transaction disputed for admin review AND broadcasts a live transaction.disputed event to connected admin WebSocket sessions.
Buyer-only, and only while still pending_payment — once paid, resolution requires an admin action (release/refund), never a direct cancel.

NOT for manual testing — Paystack calls this directly, authenticated by HMAC-SHA512 signature (not a bearer token) over the raw request body. Included here for completeness/documentation only. Idempotent: replaying a webhook for an already-processed transaction is a no-op.

item.name: Reviews
item.description: Buyer and seller rate each other after a transaction completes. Feeds User.avgRating/reviewCount and the trust score formula.

request.description: Only once the transaction is completed, only by the buyer or seller on it, one review per reviewer per transaction (409 on a repeat). role/reviewee are derived server-side from the transaction, never trusted from the body.
Reviews received by that user (as reviewee) — open to any logged-in user, since a public rating history is the point.
Both reviews on a deal (if they exist) — only visible to the buyer/seller on that transaction.

item.name: Notifications
item.description: FCM push for mobile (buyers/sellers). Admin-only live in-app alerts are a separate WebSocket channel — see the Realtime (Admin WebSocket) folder. A push failing never breaks the underlying operation (payment released, offer accepted, etc.).

request.description: Batch endpoint — tokens is an array of 1-10 { token, platform? } entries (platform is optional, ios|android), so a client can register/re-sync several device tokens in one call. Each entry upserts by token — re-registering the same device (even under a different account) moves it to the current caller. Returns { registered: <count> }.
Scoped to the caller's own tokens — deleting a token you don't own is a silent no-op, not an error.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Acting ON the marketplace (users/listings/transactions/offers/reviews) plus platform-wide App Settings — requires AdminJwtAuthGuard (the separate admin JWT, see the Admin Auth folder for how to obtain one). There is no "promote a user" path anymore — Admin is a wholly separate collection from User.

request.description: status: unverified|pending|verified|rejected. Setting verified also triggers a trust score recalculation (the same +20 flat bonus KycService.verify() applies automatically on a real provider pass).
Unlike the public search, includes archived and deleted listings.
confirmationCode is always stripped, even for admins — "only ever returned to the buyer" holds with no exception.
Only valid on stalled/disputed transactions (400 otherwise). Triggers the same Paystack payout path as a normal code confirmation, minus commission, then marks completed.
Only valid on stalled/disputed transactions (400 otherwise). reason is optional, stored on the audit log entry.
Removes an abusive/violating review and recalculates the affected user's avgRating/reviewCount and trust score.

Platform-wide tunables, DB-backed (no redeploy needed to change): escrowStalledThresholdDays, commissionPercentage, offerExpiryDays, defaultSearchRadiusKm, maxCodeAttempts. The very first call ever made creates the singleton document with defaults (5 / 10 / 3 / 15 / 3) if it doesn't exist yet — no separate seed step.

Partial update — every field optional, only send what you're changing. Bounds: commissionPercentage 0-100, the three day-counts are integers >=1, defaultSearchRadiusKm > 0. A Transaction snapshots commissionPercentage at checkout time, so changing it here never retroactively affects an in-flight or historical transaction.

item.name: Admin Auth
item.description: Admin identity/session lifecycle — separate collection, separate JWT secrets, separate password-reset flow from regular Users. This is the ONLY place admin accounts are created, logged in, or have their credentials changed.

request.description: Admin is a wholly separate Mongoose collection from User, with its own JWT secrets — structurally impossible to forge an admin token from a regular-user token or vice versa. Bootstrap the very first admin with `npm run seed:admin -- <email> <password> [name]` (one-time manual step, since no admin exists yet to create one via the API). Auto-saves adminAccessToken/adminRefreshToken.
Same rotation/revocation-detection behavior as the regular-user refresh endpoint, scoped to the separate AdminRefreshToken collection.

Any authenticated admin can create another — flat permissions, no RBAC tiers in v1. This directly creates a new Admin document (there is no "promote a user" flow). createdBy is set to the creating admin's id, for audit purposes only.
Identical stateless OTP-token design as the regular-user flow, signed with the separate JWT_ADMIN_PASSWORD_RESET_SECRET. Auto-saves adminOtpToken.

Self-invalidates via a password-hash fingerprint embedded in resetToken — reusing it after a successful reset (or after any other password change) correctly 401s. Revokes all of this admin's existing refresh tokens.

item.name: Realtime (Admin WebSocket)
item.description: Admin-only live in-app notifications over WebSocket — confirmed in scope as admin-only, not regular-user-facing (mobile stays on FCM push). See CLAUDE.md's "Admin Live Notifications — WebSocket" section.

request.description:
NOT an HTTP request — Postman's standard v2.1 collection format has no native Socket.IO request type, so this entry exists purely as documentation (running it will 404 against {{baseUrl}}/admin-notifications, that's expected/harmless — the URL field above is just a placeholder, not the real connection endpoint).

Connect with a Socket.IO client to: `{{wsBaseUrl}}` (no /api prefix — setGlobalPrefix only affects the HTTP router, not Socket.IO namespaces) namespace `/admin-notifications` (e.g. `io("{{wsBaseUrl}}/admin-notifications", { auth: { token: adminAccessToken } })`).

Auth happens at the handshake: pass the admin access token via `auth: { token }` (or an `Authorization: Bearer` header) — verified against the same JWT_ADMIN_ACCESS_SECRET as every other admin route. A missing/invalid/non-admin token gets force-disconnected immediately, before joining the broadcast room.

Events broadcast to all connected admin sessions: `transaction.disputed` ({ transactionId, buyer, seller, amount, attempts }) and `transaction.stalled` ({ transactionId, buyer, seller, amount, thresholdDays }).

If using Postman's desktop app's dedicated Socket.IO request type (separate from this REST collection), point it at the wsBaseUrl/namespace/auth above manually.
